title: Ch 7.3 — Encapsulation: Weapon with Validation
description:
Practice encapsulation: private fields, getters, and setters with rules.

──────────────────────────────────────────────
Class: Weapon (public)
──────────────────────────────────────────────

  Private fields:
    • private String name
    • private int damage

  Constructor:
    Weapon(String name, int damage)
      — Use `this` to assign both fields.
      — Clamp damage into the range 0–100:
          if damage < 0, set to 0
          if damage > 100, set to 100

  Getter:
    • public String getName()   — returns name
    • public int getDamage()    — returns damage

  Setter:
    • public void setDamage(int newDamage)
        — Same clamping rule: 0–100.
        — If newDamage < 0, set damage to 0.
        — If newDamage > 100, set damage to 100.
        — Otherwise, set damage to newDamage.

  Method:
    • public String describe()
        returns: "<n> deals <damage> damage."
        example: "Sword deals 25 damage."

──────────────────────────────────────────────
HINTS
──────────────────────────────────────────────
• Fields MUST be private — the tests check this with reflection.
• The clamping logic is the same in both the constructor and
  the setter. You could even call setDamage() from the
  constructor to avoid duplicating the if/else.
• describe() should RETURN a String, not print it.
